_comment: VC-investor-tailored cut of master.json. Leads with sourcing/diligence and investment judgment; trims back-office detail. Built by /tailor-resume.
name: Adam Breaux
contact: 713.302.2020  |  abreaux015@gmail.com  |  linkedin.com/in/adambreaux  |  Tulsa, OK (open to hybrid + travel)
summary: Investor-operator who evaluates businesses end to end — why the demand exists, how the model wins, and whether the numbers hold. Sourced and diligenced deals for a multi-billion-dollar foundation's VC arm, built three companies from 0 to 1, and ran fund operations and LP reporting. Deep financial modeling, relentless execution, and rare fluency with modern AI tooling.

items.title: Venture Fellow / Investment Diligence
items.org: Atento Capital  —  VC arm of the George Kaiser Family Foundation
bullets: Sourced 14 of 20 finalists and 4 of 7 startups selected to pitch — the most of any of ~12 fellows.
Ran screening calls, diligenced founding teams, assessed commercial viability, and presented investment theses to internal committees.
Recalled by principals to support diligence on two Core Fund I deals: bottom-up market sizing, competitive analysis, and financial-projection review delivered directly to a GP.
Identified key risks and growth levers in early-stage models and prepared briefs that informed partner decisions.

items.title: Associate (Chief of Staff to CEO / Venture Operations Lead)
bullets: Led Venture 5 from a blank page to a funded company: discovery, expert interviews, product ideation, team hiring, first customer contracts, and seed fundraising.
Built financial models and competitive analyses across 500+ competitors; computed fund TVPI for fundraising.
Built fund operations from scratch — venture- and studio-level metrics, stakeholder reporting, and LP reporting.
Developed the Fund II narrative, pitch outline, and target-investor database; built the seed-VC networking initiative on a HubSpot CRM.

items.title: Founding President
items.org: starTUp — University of Tulsa Venture Capital Club
bullets: Founded TU's first VC club; placed members as analysts on the 46VC Hurricane Venture Fund (multi-million-dollar student/alumni fund).

items.title: Chair, Macroeconomic Committee
items.org: University of Tulsa Student Investment Fund  —  $8M student-run fund
bullets: Implemented an ETF strategy and a decision-making process still in use today; made buy/sell calls on macro trends and led analysts.

items.title: Master of Business Administration — Finance & Entrepreneurship
B.S. Exercise Science / Pre-Med
items.org: The University of Tulsa  —  Division I Cross Country & Track and Field
